narration: Define a class named A with a public void show method that prints 'hi' 10 times using a for loop.
Define a class named B with a public void show method that prints 'hello' 10 times using a for loop.
Create an object of class A and assign it to a variable.
Create an object of class B and assign it to a variable.
Call the show method on both objects sequentially.
Explain that the main method pauses while a thread executes its show method.
Explain that the main method resumes only after the show method completes.
Explain that to run methods in parallel, the classes must extend the Thread class.
Extend class A to inherit from Thread.
Extend class B to inherit from Thread.
Explain that calling start() on a Thread object creates a new thread and begins execution.
Call the start() method on both thread objects to begin execution.
Explain that calling start() internally invokes the run() method.
Explain that the scheduler allows multiple threads to run in parallel by time-sharing CPU cores.
Increase the loop count to 100 to observe parallel execution more clearly.
Explain that the output shows interleaved 'hi' and 'hello' prints due to parallel execution.
Explain that the OS scheduler decides which thread gets CPU time based on available cores.
Summarize how to create multiple threads using the Thread class and start() method.
